applicationGrouping.title: Application Buckets
applicationGrouping.description: This page helps you organize applications into logical buckets for planning modernization initiatives based on various criteria such as business priority, technical complexity, and modernization approach.
applicationGrouping.bucketNameDescription: A unique identifier for the group
applicationGrouping.pilotApplicationDescription: The reference application that defines the bucket
applicationGrouping.similarityThresholdDescription: The minimum similarity score (%) required for an application to be included in the bucket
applicationGrouping.applicationsWithSimilarity: Applications with tech stack components similar to the pilot application (above the threshold) are automatically included in the bucket.
applicationGrouping.prioritizationDescription: Group applications based on business priority and technical complexity to create a phased modernization roadmap.
applicationGrouping.resourceAllocationDescription: Allocate resources efficiently by grouping applications with similar modernization approaches.
applicationGrouping.riskManagementDescription: Balance high-risk and low-risk modernization initiatives across different phases.
applicationGrouping.dependencyManagementDescription: Group applications based on dependencies to ensure coordinated modernization.
applicationGrouping.businessValueDescription: Group by business criticality and value
applicationGrouping.dependenciesDescription: Group by technical or business dependencies
applicationGrouping.teamCapabilitiesDescription: Group by required skills and team availability
applicationGrouping.step1: View existing application buckets in the table
applicationGrouping.step2: Create a new bucket by clicking "Create bucket" or directly from the Pilot Identification page
applicationGrouping.step3: Select a pilot application that will define the bucket characteristics
applicationGrouping.step4: Set the similarity threshold (%) to control which applications are included
applicationGrouping.step5: Applications with tech stack similarity above the threshold are automatically added
applicationGrouping.step6: View bucket details to see all included applications and their tech stack information
applicationGrouping.step7: Edit buckets to adjust similarity thresholds or change pilot applications
applicationGrouping.step8: Use buckets for TCO estimation and modernization planning
applicationGrouping.pilotIdentificationDescription: Identify pilot applications and create buckets directly
applicationGrouping.tcoEstimatesDescription: Create cost estimates for application buckets
applicationGrouping.techStackSimilaritiesDescription: Identify applications with similar technology stacks

teamAnalysis.title: Team Skill Category Weighting
teamAnalysis.description: The Team Analysis page allows you to assign percentage weights to skill categories for each team, helping you understand which categories are most relevant and important for each team's role and objectives.
teamAnalysis.percentageBasedDescription: All weights are expressed as percentages (0-100%)
teamAnalysis.hundredPercentMaximumDescription: Total weight allocation per team cannot exceed 100%
teamAnalysis.flexibleDistributionDescription: You can allocate any combination that totals ≤100%
teamAnalysis.nullEmptyValuesDescription: Unassigned categories are treated as 0% weight
teamAnalysis.weightAllocationColumnDescription: Shows the current status of each team's weight distribution:
teamAnalysis.progressBarDescription: Visual representation of the total weight allocation for quick assessment.
teamAnalysis.editWeightsDescription: Click "Edit Weights" for any team to open the weighting modal
teamAnalysis.assignPercentagesDescription: Enter percentage values for relevant skill categories
teamAnalysis.monitorTotalDescription: Watch the progress bar and total allocation at the top
teamAnalysis.validateDescription: Ensure total doesn't exceed 100% (save button will be disabled if invalid)
teamAnalysis.saveChangesDescription: Click "Save Weights" to store your allocation
teamAnalysis.clearWeightsDescription: Use "Clear" button to reset a team's weights to zero
teamAnalysis.priorityIdentificationDescription: Clearly define which skill areas are most important for each team
teamAnalysis.resourcePlanningDescription: Guide training and hiring decisions based on weighted priorities
teamAnalysis.teamComparisonDescription: Compare focus areas across different teams
teamAnalysis.skillGapAnalysisDescription: Identify where teams should concentrate development efforts
teamAnalysis.performanceMetricsDescription: Use weights to create team-specific skill assessments
teamAnalysis.developmentTeamExample: Might allocate 40% to Programming, 25% to DevOps, 20% to Database Management, 15% to Cloud Computing
teamAnalysis.dataTeamExample: Could assign 35% to Database Management, 30% to Analytics, 20% to Programming, 15% to Machine Learning
teamAnalysis.infrastructureTeamExample: May focus 50% on Cloud Computing, 30% on DevOps, 20% on Security
teamAnalysis.dataPersistenceDescription: Weight allocations are stored locally and persist across sessions. In a production environment, these weights would be saved to your organization's database and could be used for reporting, analytics, and integration with other HR and project management systems.

tco.title: TCO Estimates
tco.description: This page helps you analyze and compare the total cost of ownership estimates for your application buckets to make informed modernization decisions and budget planning.
tco.costComparisonDescription: Compare the TCO across different application buckets to identify cost-saving opportunities.
tco.roiAnalysisDescription: Calculate the return on investment for modernization initiatives based on TCO estimates.
tco.budgetPlanningDescription: Plan modernization budgets based on comprehensive TCO analysis per bucket.
tco.costOptimizationDescription: Identify cost optimization opportunities across your application portfolio.
tco.computeCostsDescription: Server, virtual machine, or container costs
tco.databaseCostsDescription: Database licensing, provisioning, and maintenance costs
tco.integrationCostsDescription: API management, messaging, and connectivity costs
tco.storageCostsDescription: Block storage, object storage, and backup costs
tco.utilizationSizeDescription: T-shirt sizing (XS to XXL) to estimate resource requirements
tco.timePeriodDescription: Duration for which the TCO is calculated (hours or days)
tco.step1: View existing TCO estimates in the table or create a new one
tco.step2: To create a new TCO estimate, click the "Create TCO estimate" button
tco.step3: Select an application bucket that doesn't have a TCO estimate yet
tco.step4: Enter utilization information using T-shirt sizing (XS to XXL)
tco.step5: Specify the time period (hours or days) and duration
tco.step6: Enter cost estimates for compute, database, integration, and storage for the pilot application
tco.step7: Review the applications in the bucket with costs calculated based on similarity scores
tco.step8: Check the Cost Summary section to see total costs across all applications
tco.step9: Save the TCO estimate to add it to the table
tco.step10: You can edit or delete existing TCO estimates using the action buttons
tco.costCalculationDescription: The TCO estimate calculates costs for all applications in a bucket based on:
tco.pilotApplicationCostsDescription: The base costs you enter for the pilot application
tco.similarityScoresDescription: Each application's costs are calculated as a percentage of the pilot costs based on their similarity score
tco.aggregatedCostsDescription: The total costs shown in the TCO table represent the sum of all application costs in the bucket
tco.progressBarDescription: Visual representation of the total weight allocation for quick assessment.

instructions.assignWeights: Assign percentage weights to each skill category for every team
instructions.totalMustEqual100: Total weights for each team must equal 100%
instructions.saveChanges: Click 'Update Weights' to save your changes

visionAnalysis.description: The Vision Analysis page provides a visual representation of your organization's technology vision using a Tech Radar chart. This visualization helps stakeholders understand the current technology landscape and strategic direction for future adoption.
visionAnalysis.techRadarDescription: The Tech Radar is divided into four quadrants and four rings:
visionAnalysis.techniquesDescription: Practices and methodologies that teams use
visionAnalysis.toolsDescription: Software tools and products that help teams be productive
visionAnalysis.platformsDescription: Software platforms and services that teams build upon
visionAnalysis.languagesFrameworksDescription: Programming languages and frameworks that teams use
visionAnalysis.adoptDescription: Technologies we recommend for widespread use
visionAnalysis.trialDescription: Technologies worth exploring with the goal of understanding how they would benefit your organization
visionAnalysis.assessDescription: Technologies worth exploring to understand their impact
visionAnalysis.holdDescription: Technologies that should be avoided for new projects
visionAnalysis.clickTechnologyDescription: Click on any technology in the radar to see more details about it, including its description and current status.
visionAnalysis.strategicPlanningDescription: Use the Tech Radar to inform strategic decisions about which technologies to invest in, which to experiment with, and which to avoid. This helps align technology choices with business goals and ensures a coherent technology strategy.
visionAnalysis.skillsDevelopmentDescription: Identify technologies in the "Adopt" and "Trial" rings to prioritize for team training and skills development. This helps ensure your teams have the right skills for current and future projects.
visionAnalysis.technologyGovernanceDescription: Use the Tech Radar as a governance tool to guide technology choices across teams and projects. This helps reduce technology sprawl and promotes standardization where appropriate.
visionAnalysis.maintainingDescription: A Tech Radar should be a living document that evolves over time. Consider these best practices:
visionAnalysis.regularReviewsDescription: Review and update your Tech Radar quarterly or bi-annually
visionAnalysis.collaborativeProcessDescription: Involve technical leaders from across the organization
visionAnalysis.evidenceBasedDescription: Base decisions on actual experience with technologies
visionAnalysis.clearCriteriaDescription: Define clear criteria for moving technologies between rings
visionAnalysis.communicateChangesDescription: Clearly communicate and explain changes to stakeholders
visionAnalysis.awsCreateRadar: AWS: Create Your Own Technology Radar
visionAnalysis.buildOwnRadar: How to Build Your Own Technology Radar

technologyVision.title: Technology Vision
technologyVision.description: The Technology Vision section helps you visualize your organization's technology adoption strategy. It categorizes technologies into four quadrants and four adoption phases to provide a clear roadmap for technology decisions.
technologyVision.techniquesDescription: Approaches, methodologies, and practices that teams use
technologyVision.toolsDescription: Software tools and utilities that support development and operations
technologyVision.platformsDescription: Products or technologies that provide a foundation for applications
technologyVision.languagesFrameworksDescription: Programming languages and frameworks for development
technologyVision.adoptDescription: Technologies that are ready for enterprise use and recommended
technologyVision.trialDescription: Technologies worth pursuing with limited scope to gain experience
technologyVision.assessDescription: Technologies worth exploring to understand their impact and potential
technologyVision.holdDescription: Technologies that should be avoided for new projects
technologyVision.step1: Review the technology radar table showing all technologies and their current status
technologyVision.step2: Use filters to focus on specific quadrants or adoption phases
technologyVision.step3: Add new technologies by clicking "Add Technology"
technologyVision.step4: Edit existing technologies to update their status or description
technologyVision.step5: Use the radar visualization to get a visual overview of your technology landscape
technologyVision.step6: Export the data for sharing with stakeholders
technologyVision.regularUpdatesDescription: Review and update the technology radar quarterly
technologyVision.teamInputDescription: Involve technical teams in technology assessment decisions
technologyVision.clearCriteriaDescription: Define clear criteria for moving technologies between phases
technologyVision.documentationDescription: Document the rationale behind technology decisions
technologyVision.visionAnalysisDescription: Analyze your technology vision with interactive radar visualization

techStackAnalysis.consolidationOpportunities: Identify technologies with low usage that could be consolidated

dataProcessing.title: Data Processing
dataProcessing.description: The Data Processing page provides a comprehensive dashboard for monitoring and managing data processing operations across the App-ModEx platform.
dataProcessing.processTrackingDescription: Monitor the status of all data processing operations, including file uploads, data transformations, and analysis tasks.
dataProcessing.filteringDescription: Filter processes by date range, status, and process type to quickly find relevant information.
dataProcessing.historicalDataDescription: Access historical processing data to analyze trends and identify recurring issues.
dataProcessing.errorHandlingDescription: View detailed error information for failed processes to facilitate troubleshooting.
dataProcessing.fileUploadDescription: Tracks file uploads to the platform.
dataProcessing.dataTransformationDescription: Tracks data transformation operations, such as converting uploaded files to Athena-compatible formats.
dataProcessing.dataImportDescription: Tracks data import operations from external sources.
dataProcessing.dataExportDescription: Tracks data export operations to external destinations.
dataProcessing.dataValidationDescription: Tracks data validation operations to ensure data quality.
dataProcessing.initiatedDescription: The process has been created but has not started processing.
dataProcessing.failedDescription: The process encountered an error and could not complete.
dataProcessing.cancelledDescription: The process was cancelled by a user or system action.
dataProcessing.regularMonitoringDescription: Check the Data Processing page regularly to ensure all processes are running smoothly.
dataProcessing.errorInvestigationDescription: Investigate failed processes promptly to prevent data inconsistencies.
dataProcessing.historicalAnalysisDescription: Use historical data to identify patterns and optimize processing workflows.
dataProcessing.awsLambdaInvocationDescription: Learn more about AWS Lambda function invocation.
dataProcessing.dynamodbStreamsDescription: Learn more about DynamoDB Streams for event-driven processing.

componentSimilarities.title: Component Similarities
componentSimilarities.description: This page analyzes similarities at the individual component level, comparing each component's technology stack (runtime, framework, databases, integrations, storages) against all other components across your application portfolio.
componentSimilarities.similarityMatrixDescription: Visual matrix showing similarity scores between all component pairs
componentSimilarities.componentClustersDescription: Groups of components with similar technology stacks
componentSimilarities.repeatedPatternsDescription: Identifies the most common technology combinations for quick wins
componentSimilarities.configurableScoringDescription: Adjust weights for different technology categories
componentSimilarities.scoringDescription: Components are scored based on shared technologies:
componentSimilarities.quickWin1: Components with identical or near-identical technology stacks for consolidation
componentSimilarities.quickWin2: Most frequently used technology combinations for standardization
componentSimilarities.quickWin3: Outlier components that may need special migration attention
componentSimilarities.quickWin4: Opportunities to create reusable component templates
componentSimilarities.quickWin5: Technology patterns that can be replicated across teams
componentSimilarities.learnMoreDescription: For more information about component-level analysis and modernization strategies:

instructions.clickCards: Click on cards to navigate to specific pages

instructions.chooseComplexity: Choose the project complexity and time period for the estimate

exportData.title: Export Project Data
exportData.description: The Export Data page allows you to selectively export data from different sections of your modernization project. Choose which sections to include in your export to create customized reports and data packages.
exportData.dataSectionsDescription: Raw data collected for your project
exportData.skillsDescription: Team skills inventory and proficiency levels
exportData.technologyVisionDescription: Target technology stack and adoption phases
exportData.applicationsDescription: Application portfolio, tech stack, infrastructure, and utilization data
exportData.skillsAnalysisDescription: Skill gap analysis and team comparisons
exportData.selectSectionsDescription: Check the boxes for sections you want to include in your export
exportData.useQuickActionsDescription: Click "Select All" to include everything, or "Deselect All" to start fresh
exportData.reviewSelectionDescription: Ensure you've selected all desired sections
exportData.exportDescription: Click "Export Selected" to generate your data package
exportData.selectiveExportDescription: Choose only the sections you need
exportData.currentFiltersAppliedDescription: Exports respect any filters you've applied in each section
exportData.projectSpecificDescription: Exports data for the currently selected project only
exportData.multipleFormatsDescription: Data can be exported in various formats (implementation pending)
exportData.executiveReportsDescription: Export insights and planning sections for stakeholder presentations
exportData.dataBackupDescription: Export all sections to create a complete project snapshot
exportData.focusedAnalysisDescription: Export specific sections for detailed offline analysis
exportData.teamSharingDescription: Export relevant sections to share with different teams
exportData.complianceDescription: Export data sections for audit and compliance purposes
exportData.tip1: All sections are selected by default for convenience
exportData.tip2: Deselect sections you don't need to reduce export size
exportData.tip3: Applied filters in each section will be reflected in the export
exportData.tip4: Export functionality will be fully implemented in a future release

instructions.selectCategories: Select the data categories you want to include in your export

iac.title: Infrastructure as Code (IaC)
iac.description: This page helps you manage and track the implementation of Infrastructure as Code for your modernized applications, ensuring consistent, repeatable, and version-controlled infrastructure deployments.
iac.consistencyDesc: Ensure consistent infrastructure deployments across environments and applications.
iac.versionControlDesc: Track changes to infrastructure configurations over time with full version history.
iac.automationDesc: Automate infrastructure provisioning and updates to reduce manual errors and improve efficiency.
iac.documentationDesc: Self-documenting infrastructure configurations that serve as living documentation.
iac.complianceDesc: Enforce security and compliance requirements through code-based policies and guardrails.
iac.cloudFormationDesc: Native AWS service for infrastructure provisioning
iac.awsCdkDesc: Cloud Development Kit for defining infrastructure using familiar programming languages
iac.pulumiDesc: Modern infrastructure as code using programming languages
iac.ansibleDesc: Configuration management and application deployment tool
iac.targetArchitectureDesc: View the target architecture that guides your IaC implementation
iac.infrastructureDesc: View detailed infrastructure data for your applications

targetArchitecture.title: Target Architecture
targetArchitecture.description: This page helps you define, document, and track the implementation of your target architecture for application modernization.
targetArchitecture.architectureDocumentationDesc: Document your target architecture patterns and principles for consistent implementation.
targetArchitecture.implementationTrackingDesc: Track the progress of architecture implementation across your application portfolio.
targetArchitecture.referenceArchitectureDesc: Provide reference architectures for different application types and use cases.
targetArchitecture.governanceDesc: Ensure architectural compliance and consistency across modernization initiatives.
targetArchitecture.technologyVisionDesc: View the technology vision that guides your target architecture
targetArchitecture.techStackSimilaritiesDesc: Identify applications with similar technology stacks for coordinated modernization

dataDivergencies.title: Data Divergencies Analysis
dataDivergencies.description: This page helps you identify inconsistencies between different data sources in the App-ModEx platform. Divergencies occur when an application exists in one data source but is missing in others.
dataDivergencies.whyImportantDesc: Data consistency is crucial for accurate modernization planning. Divergencies can lead to:
dataDivergencies.missingApplications: Missing applications in your modernization plan
dataDivergencies.errorDesc: Missing in Portfolio (critical issue) - Applications that exist in other data sources but are not registered in the application portfolio. These should be addressed immediately.
dataDivergencies.warningDesc: Missing in other data sources - Applications that exist in one data source but are missing in others. These should be investigated.
dataDivergencies.portfolioDivergenciesDesc: Applications in the portfolio that are missing in other data sources. Ensure these applications have complete data in all systems.
dataDivergencies.techStackDivergenciesDesc: Applications in the tech stack that are missing in other data sources. If missing from the portfolio, add them immediately.
dataDivergencies.infrastructureDivergenciesDesc: Applications in the infrastructure that are missing in other data sources. If missing from the portfolio, add them immediately.
dataDivergencies.utilizationDivergenciesDesc: Applications in the utilization data that are missing in other data sources. If missing from the portfolio, add them immediately.
dataDivergencies.regularReview: Regularly review and resolve data divergencies
dataDivergencies.prioritizePortfolio: Prioritize applications missing from the portfolio
dataDivergencies.establishProcess: Establish a process for maintaining data consistency across systems
dataDivergencies.documentReasons: Document reasons for intentional divergencies (e.g., applications being phased out)
dataDivergencies.needHelpDesc: For more information on managing data consistency, refer to the

projectStatus.projectLifecycleDescription: Projects go through different states during their lifecycle. Understanding these states helps you know when projects can be deleted and what to expect during operations.
projectStatus.pendingDescription: Project created and waiting for provisioning to begin
projectStatus.provisioningDescription: Infrastructure is being deployed via CloudFormation
projectStatus.activeDescription: Project successfully provisioned and ready for use
projectStatus.failedToProvisionDescription: Provisioning automation failed - may have partial infrastructure
projectStatus.failedToDeleteDescription: Deletion automation failed - infrastructure likely still exists
projectStatus.failedWithStackDescription: Failed project with confirmed existing CloudFormation stack
projectStatus.activeProjectsDescription: Infrastructure will be cleanly destroyed via CloudFormation before removing the project record.
projectStatus.failedToProvisionRuleDescription: System checks for existing infrastructure. If found, you'll see details and can choose to delete anyway.
projectStatus.failedToDeleteRuleDescription: Deletion automation failed, infrastructure likely still exists. You'll see stack details before proceeding.
projectStatus.failedWithStackRuleDescription: Confirmed existing infrastructure. You'll see stack details and can choose to delete project record anyway.
projectStatus.pendingProvisioningProjectsDescription: Cannot be deleted while operations are in progress. Wait for completion or failure.
projectStatus.deletingProjectsDescription: Already being deleted. The process will complete automatically.
projectStatus.additionalInformationDescription: Project resources are automatically managed by the system. If you encounter any issues with project provisioning or deletion, please contact your administrator for assistance.

utilizationInfo.title: Resource Utilization Information
utilizationInfo.description: The Resource Utilization page provides insights into how your infrastructure resources are being used. Use this data to identify optimization opportunities and right-sizing candidates.
utilizationInfo.timestampDescription: When the utilization data was collected
utilizationInfo.cpuUtilizationDescription: Percentage of CPU capacity being used
utilizationInfo.memoryUtilizationDescription: Percentage of memory capacity being used
utilizationInfo.storageUtilizationDescription: Percentage of storage capacity being used
utilizationInfo.iopsDescription: Input/Output Operations Per Second for storage
utilizationInfo.notesDescription: Additional information about the utilization data
utilizationInfo.highDescription: Resource is heavily utilized and may need scaling
utilizationInfo.bestPractice1: Monitor utilization trends over time to identify patterns
utilizationInfo.bestPractice2: Right-size resources based on actual utilization data
utilizationInfo.bestPractice3: Set up alerts for high utilization thresholds
utilizationInfo.bestPractice4: Consider auto-scaling for resources with variable workloads
utilizationInfo.bestPractice5: Investigate consistently low utilization for cost optimization

default.title: Information
default.description: Select a specific section to see detailed information and guidance.

projectHome.title: Project Home
projectHome.description: Welcome to your project home page. This is the central hub for your modernization assessment project. From here, you can navigate to different sections of the application to collect data, analyze insights, plan your modernization initiatives, and track execution.
projectHome.dataCollectionDescription: Collect and organize information about your current state, including team skills, technology vision, application portfolio, technology stack, infrastructure resources, and resource utilization metrics.
projectHome.insightsDescription: Analyze the collected data to identify modernization opportunities, including skill gaps analysis, technology stack assessment, and infrastructure insights.
projectHome.planningDescription: Organize and prioritize modernization initiatives using pilot identification, application buckets, and Total Cost of Ownership (TCO) analysis. Identify ideal POC candidates and group similar applications for efficient modernization planning.
projectHome.navigationTip1: Use the navigation sidebar to quickly access different sections
projectHome.navigationTip2: Click on cards to navigate to specific pages
projectHome.navigationTip3: Use the "Back to Projects" button to return to the projects list
projectHome.navigationTip4: Click on the project name in the top navigation to switch projects
projectHome.bestPractice1: Start by collecting data about your current state
projectHome.bestPractice2: Use the insights section to identify modernization opportunities
projectHome.bestPractice3: Create a plan based on the identified opportunities
projectHome.bestPractice5: Regularly update your data to keep your assessment current

vision.title: Technology Vision Information
vision.description: The Technology Vision page provides a visual representation of your technology adoption strategy using an interactive radar visualization and detailed table view.
vision.keyInformation: Key information about the technology radar:
vision.techniquesDescription: Methods and processes for software development and operations
vision.toolsDescription: Software used to develop, deploy, and operate applications
vision.platformsDescription: Products and services that host applications and data
vision.languagesFrameworksDescription: Programming languages and frameworks for building applications
vision.trialDescription: Technologies being evaluated in limited scope
vision.holdDescription: Technologies to avoid or phase out

projects.title: Projects
projects.description: App-ModEx allows you to manage multiple modernization assessment projects. Each project represents a separate modernization initiative with its own data and analysis.
projects.creatingProjectDescription: Click the "Create project" button and provide a name and optional notes for your new project. Once created, you'll be automatically directed to the project home page.
projects.selectingProjectDescription: Click on a project name in the table to select it and navigate to its home page. The navigation sidebar will expand once a project is selected.
projects.deletingProjectDescription: Click the "Delete" action for a project to remove it. This action cannot be undone, so be careful when deleting projects.
projects.switchingProjectsDescription: When working within a project, you can switch to another project by clicking the "Switch Project" button in the navigation sidebar or using the project name in the top navigation.
projects.projectDataDescription: Each project contains its own set of data across the following categories:
projects.teamSkillsDescription: Inventory of team skills and capabilities
projects.applicationPortfolioDescription: Catalog of applications with criticality assessment
projects.bestPractice1: Create separate projects for distinct modernization initiatives
projects.bestPractice2: Use descriptive project names that clearly identify the scope
projects.bestPractice3: Include relevant context in the project notes
projects.bestPractice4: Regularly review and clean up unused projects

techStack.title: Tech Stack Information
techStack.description: The Tech Stack page provides details about the technology components used across your applications. Use this data to identify technology standardization opportunities and modernization targets.
techStack.componentNameDescription: The name of the specific component within the application
techStack.runtimeDescription: The runtime environment used by the component (one per component)
techStack.databaseDescription: The database technologies used by the component (can be multiple)
techStack.integrationDescription: The integration technologies used by the component (can be multiple)
techStack.storageDescription: The storage solutions used by the component (can be multiple)
techStack.bestPractice1: Standardize on a limited set of technologies to reduce complexity
techStack.bestPractice2: Identify outdated or unsupported technologies for modernization
techStack.bestPractice3: Consolidate similar technologies to reduce operational overhead

infrastructure.title: Infrastructure Information
infrastructure.description: The Infrastructure page provides details about your current infrastructure resources. Use this data to identify infrastructure modernization opportunities and plan cloud migrations.
infrastructure.applicationNameDescription: The name of the application using this resource
infrastructure.serverNameDescription: The unique identifier for this server
infrastructure.serverTypeDescription: The EC2 instance type (t3.medium, m5.large, etc.)
infrastructure.cpuDescription: The CPU allocation for this resource
infrastructure.memoryDescription: The memory allocation for this resource
infrastructure.regionLocationDescription: The AWS region or data center location
infrastructure.bestPractice1: Regularly review resource utilization to identify optimization opportunities
infrastructure.bestPractice2: Consider right-sizing instances based on actual usage patterns
infrastructure.bestPractice3: Implement infrastructure as code for consistent deployments
infrastructure.bestPractice4: Use tagging strategies to track resource ownership and purpose
infrastructure.bestPractice5: Plan for high availability across multiple regions or availability zones

skills.title: Team Skills
skills.description: The Team Skills page provides a comprehensive inventory of skills across your organization, helping you understand your team's capabilities and identify potential skill gaps.
skills.viewAllSkills: View all skills in your organization
skills.filterSkills: Filter skills by name, category, proficiency level, or team
skills.downloadSkillsData: Download skills data for further analysis
skills.proficiencyLevelsDescription: The proficiency levels are color-coded for easy identification:
skills.bestPracticesDescription: Consider these best practices when managing your skills inventory:
skills.bestPractice1: Regularly update skill proficiency levels as team members gain experience
skills.bestPractice2: Use consistent categories to group related skills
skills.bestPractice3: Include both technical and non-technical skills
skills.bestPractice4: Add detailed notes to provide context about how skills are used
skills.bestPractice5: Identify skill gaps by comparing your inventory with technology requirements

techStackAnalysis.title: Tech Stack Analysis
techStackAnalysis.description: This page provides visualizations and analysis of your technology stack components. Use these insights to identify standardization opportunities, technology consolidation candidates, and modernization priorities.
techStackAnalysis.componentsPerApplicationDescription: This chart shows the number of components per application. Applications with many components may be candidates for decomposition or microservice refactoring.
techStackAnalysis.runtimeDistributionDescription: This chart shows the distribution of runtime technologies across your components. A high diversity of runtimes may indicate opportunities for standardization.
techStackAnalysis.databaseDistributionDescription: This chart shows the distribution of database technologies across your components. Consider consolidating database technologies to reduce operational complexity.
techStackAnalysis.integrationDistributionDescription: This chart shows the distribution of integration technologies across your components. Standardizing on fewer integration patterns can improve maintainability.
techStackAnalysis.storageDistributionDescription: This chart shows the distribution of storage technologies across your components. Consider consolidating storage solutions to optimize costs and management.
techStackAnalysis.recommendedAction1: Identify technologies with low usage that could be consolidated
techStackAnalysis.recommendedAction2: Standardize on preferred technologies for each category
techStackAnalysis.recommendedAction3: Create migration plans for components using outdated technologies
techStackAnalysis.recommendedAction4: Develop governance policies for introducing new technologies
techStackAnalysis.migrationStrategies: 6 Strategies for Migrating Applications to the Cloud

skillsAnalysis.aboutDescription: The Skills Analysis page provides a visual representation of the gap between each team's actual skill proficiency levels and the required levels needed for optimal performance. This analysis helps identify training needs and resource allocation priorities.
skillsAnalysis.heatmapDescription: The heatmap displays the absolute difference between actual and required skill levels:
skillsAnalysis.heatmapLevel0Description: The team's skill level matches the required level
skillsAnalysis.heatmapLevel1Description: Small gap that may need attention
skillsAnalysis.heatmapLevel2Description: Moderate gap that should be addressed
skillsAnalysis.heatmapLevel3Description: Significant gap that requires immediate action
skillsAnalysis.heatmapUsage: The heatmap shows teams on the Y-axis and individual skills on the X-axis. Click on any cell to view detailed information about that team's proficiency in all skills.
skillsAnalysis.identifyTrainingNeedsDescription: Use the skills gap analysis to identify specific skills where teams need additional training to reach their required proficiency levels.
skillsAnalysis.resourceAllocationDescription: Prioritize resource allocation based on the criticality of skill gaps and their impact on project delivery and quality.
skillsAnalysis.strategicPlanningDescription: Use the gap analysis to inform strategic decisions about hiring, training programs, and team composition to address critical skill shortages.
skillsAnalysis.tableDescription: The Team Skill Details table below the heatmap provides a detailed breakdown of each team's skills:
skillsAnalysis.teamDescription: The team or persona responsible for the skill
skillsAnalysis.skillDescription: The specific technical or professional skill being assessed
skillsAnalysis.categoryDescription: The category the skill belongs to (e.g., Frontend, Backend, Cloud)
skillsAnalysis.actualLevelDescription: The current proficiency level of the team (1-5 scale)
skillsAnalysis.requiredLevelDescription: The proficiency level required for optimal performance (1-5 scale)
skillsAnalysis.gapDescription: The difference between actual and required levels
skillsAnalysis.statusDescription: An assessment of the gap (Aligned, Exceeds, Needs Improvement)
skillsAnalysis.filteringDescription: You can filter the table by selecting specific teams from the dropdown menu. The selected teams will also be highlighted in the heatmap visualization for easier correlation.
skillsAnalysis.noviceDescription: Basic awareness but limited practical experience
skillsAnalysis.beginnerDescription: Can perform simple tasks with guidance
skillsAnalysis.intermediateDescription: Can work independently on routine tasks
skillsAnalysis.advancedDescription: Deep knowledge and can handle complex scenarios
skillsAnalysis.expertDescription: Comprehensive mastery and can innovate/teach others
skillsAnalysis.awsSkillsGapBlog: AWS Skills Gap Analysis Blog Posts

teamEstimate.title: Team Estimates
teamEstimate.description: The Team Estimates page allows you to create and manage team resource estimates for your application buckets. This helps in planning the people, skills, and time needed for your modernization projects.
teamEstimate.peoplePlanningDescription: Estimate the number of people needed for different roles
teamEstimate.skillsAssessmentDescription: Define required skills and expertise levels
teamEstimate.timeEstimationDescription: Calculate project duration and effort in person-hours/days
teamEstimate.bucketBasedEstimatesDescription: Create estimates for application buckets with pilot applications
teamEstimate.roleBasedPlanningDescription: Estimate resources for developers, architects, testers, and other roles
teamEstimate.applicationScalingDescription: Automatically calculate resources for similar applications based on similarity scores
teamEstimate.step1: Click "Create Team Estimate" to start a new estimate
teamEstimate.step2: Select an application bucket that doesn't already have a team estimate
teamEstimate.step3: Choose the project complexity and time period for the estimate
teamEstimate.step4: Enter resource requirements for different roles (developers, architects, testers, etc.)
teamEstimate.step5: Define required skills and experience levels
teamEstimate.step6: Review the calculated resources for similar applications in the bucket
teamEstimate.step7: Save the estimate to track human resource planning
teamEstimate.developersDescription: Number of developers needed and their skill levels
teamEstimate.projectDurationDescription: Estimated time in weeks or months

pilotIdentification.title: Pilot Identification
pilotIdentification.description: The Pilot Identification page helps you identify ideal POC candidates for your modernization initiatives based on business drivers, compelling events, and application characteristics.
pilotIdentification.businessDriverSelectionDescription: Align your modernization efforts with key business objectives such as cost reduction, agility, operational efficiency, and risk reduction.
pilotIdentification.compellingEventIdentificationDescription: Factor in time-sensitive events like data center exits, license renewals, hardware refreshes, and end-of-support deadlines.
pilotIdentification.teamCapabilityMatchingDescription: Find applications that align with your team's existing technical skills to reduce the learning curve.
pilotIdentification.applicationGroupingDescription: Identify groups of similar applications that could benefit from the same modernization approach. Use the "Create Bucket with this Pilot" button to seamlessly create application buckets.
pilotIdentification.advancedFilteringDescription: Fine-tune your search with customizable weights, risk tolerance, and similarity thresholds.
pilotIdentification.step1Description: Choose the primary business objectives driving your modernization initiative.
pilotIdentification.step2Description: Select any time-sensitive events that create urgency for modernization.
pilotIdentification.step3Description: Adjust team capabilities, risk tolerance, and similarity thresholds as needed.
pilotIdentification.step4Description: Click "Find Candidates" to identify potential pilot applications based on your criteria.
pilotIdentification.step5Description: Examine the candidate applications and their details to make informed decisions.
pilotIdentification.step6Description: Use "Create Bucket with this Pilot" to group similar applications for modernization planning.
pilotIdentification.startSmallDescription: Choose a non-critical application with moderate complexity for your initial POC to demonstrate value while minimizing risk.
pilotIdentification.considerDependenciesDescription: Applications with fewer external dependencies are typically easier to modernize.
pilotIdentification.balanceFactorsDescription: The ideal POC candidate has both strong business alignment and technical feasibility.
pilotIdentification.lookForPatternsDescription: Identify applications with similar technology stacks to create reusable modernization patterns.
pilotIdentification.validateWithStakeholdersDescription: Ensure business and technical stakeholders agree on the selected POC candidates.
pilotIdentification.sixStrategies: 6 Strategies for Migrating Applications to the Cloud
pilotIdentification.prioritizeApplications: Prioritize Applications for Migration to AWS Cloud

infrastructureAnalysis.title: Infrastructure Analysis
infrastructureAnalysis.description: This page provides insights into your infrastructure resources across your application portfolio. Use these visualizations to identify patterns, optimization opportunities, and modernization candidates.
infrastructureAnalysis.resourceDistributionDescription: Analyze the distribution of server types, operating systems, and database engines across your infrastructure.
infrastructureAnalysis.environmentAnalysisDescription: Compare resource allocation across different environments (Production, Staging, Development).
infrastructureAnalysis.regionalDistributionDescription: Visualize how your infrastructure is distributed across AWS regions.
infrastructureAnalysis.filtersDescription: Use the filters at the top to focus on specific applications, environments, or resource types.
infrastructureAnalysis.interactiveChartsDescription: Click on chart segments to filter the data and explore specific aspects of your infrastructure.
infrastructureAnalysis.summaryMetricsDescription: Review the summary section for key statistics and insights about your infrastructure.

utilizationAnalysis.title: Utilization Analysis
utilizationAnalysis.description: This page provides insights into resource utilization trends across your application portfolio. Use these visualizations to identify patterns, optimize resource allocation, and plan capacity.
utilizationAnalysis.utilizationTrendsDescription: Analyze CPU, memory, and storage utilization trends over time to identify patterns and anomalies.
utilizationAnalysis.resourceOptimizationDescription: Identify over-provisioned or under-provisioned resources to optimize costs and performance.
utilizationAnalysis.capacityPlanningDescription: Use historical utilization data to forecast future resource needs and plan capacity accordingly.
utilizationAnalysis.performanceAnalysisDescription: Correlate utilization metrics with application performance to identify bottlenecks.
utilizationAnalysis.metricSelectionDescription: Use the tabs to switch between different utilization metrics (CPU, Memory, Storage, Network, IOPS).
utilizationAnalysis.timePeriodDescription: Select different time periods to analyze utilization trends over different timeframes.
utilizationAnalysis.applicationSelectionDescription: Filter the data to focus on specific applications or compare utilization across multiple applications.
utilizationAnalysis.interactiveChartsDescription: Hover over data points to see detailed information and click on legend items to toggle visibility.
utilizationAnalysis.utilizationDataDescription: View detailed utilization data for all resources

pipelines.title: CI/CD Pipelines
pipelines.description: This page helps you manage and track the implementation of CI/CD pipelines for your modernized applications, ensuring automated, reliable, and consistent deployment processes.
pipelines.automationDesc: Automate build, test, and deployment processes to reduce manual errors and improve efficiency.
pipelines.consistencyDesc: Ensure consistent deployment processes across environments and applications.
pipelines.reliabilityDesc: Improve deployment reliability through automated testing and validation.
pipelines.visibilityDesc: Gain visibility into deployment status and history across your application portfolio.
pipelines.governanceDesc: Enforce security and compliance requirements through automated checks and approvals.
pipelines.buildDesc: Compilation and packaging of application code
pipelines.testDesc: Automated unit, integration, and acceptance testing
pipelines.targetArchitectureDesc: View the target architecture that guides your pipeline implementation
pipelines.infrastructureAsCodeDesc: View the IaC implementation that supports your pipelines

portfolio.title: Application Portfolio Information
portfolio.description: The Application Portfolio page provides an overview of all applications in your organization. Use this data to understand your current application landscape and identify modernization candidates.
portfolio.purposeDesc: The business purpose of the application
portfolio.criticalityDesc: The business criticality level (Critical, High, Medium, Low)
portfolio.departmentDesc: The department responsible for the application
portfolio.practice4: Prioritize modernization efforts based on criticality

applicationSimilarities.title: Application Similarities
applicationSimilarities.description: This page will visualize application clusters based on similarity scores. Applications will be grouped together based on their technology stack similarities, helping you identify standardization opportunities and potential consolidation candidates.
applicationSimilarities.benefitsDescription: Identifying application clusters with similar technology stacks can help you:
applicationSimilarities.benefit1: Standardize technology stacks across similar applications
applicationSimilarities.benefit2: Identify consolidation opportunities to reduce maintenance overhead
applicationSimilarities.benefit3: Plan migration waves based on technology similarities
applicationSimilarities.benefit4: Optimize resource allocation by grouping similar applications
applicationSimilarities.benefit5: Identify outliers that may require special handling during modernization
applicationSimilarities.learnMoreDescription: For more information about application portfolio rationalization and technology standardization, check out these resources:
applicationSimilarities.migrationStrategies: 6 Strategies for Migrating Applications to the Cloud
applicationSimilarities.sixRsStrategies: The 6 Rs of Cloud Migration Strategies
